repeatedUiMarkupAndCss.decision: retain-local; sharing would add coupling and may increase every bundle

accepted.area: add-on API wrappers and domain selectors
accepted.rationale: Keeping these local avoids coupling and prevents shared code from entering every bundle.
Hybrid external-host behavior has distinct lifecycle and security ownership.
The generated current feed and legacy service-folder publication serve different release compatibility paths.
Small per-add-on adaptors preserve explicit ownership while shared transport remains tree-shakeable.
Repeated export names are compatibility projections, not duplicated runtime implementations; removing them would change established import boundaries.

futureReductions.evidence: Review facade exports with zero source callers before removal; preserve public import compatibility until release policy permits it.
Investigate one-consumer helpers only when a measured bundle reduction is demonstrated; maintain explicit lifecycle ownership.
Review one-consumer action/API candidates against the accepted public contract; no API is removed here.
Start with the largest release contributors named per add-on; preserve behavior and measure gzip as informational.
Registration transport/security remains unchanged and outside this plan.

optionalTrendBudgets.rationale: Recommendation only; no gate is enabled by this package.
